steps.title: Solve the system by substitution
steps.content: From the first equation, $y=-x^2$. Substitute into $20x+100-y=0$: $$20x+100+x^2 = 0 \Rightarrow (x+10)^2 = 0.$$

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: The correct answer is $-10$. Adding $y$ to both sides of the second equation in the given system yields $20x+100=y$. Substituting $20x+100$ for $y$ in the first equation in the given system yields ${x}^{2}+20x+100+7=7$. Subtracting $7$ from both sides of this equation yields ${x}^{2}+20x+100=0$. Factoring the left-hand side of this equation yields $\left(x+10\right)\left(x+10\right)=0$, or ${\left(x+10\right)}^{2}=0$. Taking the square root of both sides of this equation yields $x+10=0$. Subtracting $10$ from both sides of this equation yields $x=-10$. Therefore, the value of $x$ is $-10$.
